project.name: TheRock
project.description: A CMake super-project for building HIP and ROCm from source. Analysis scoped to the core build system: Python build_tools/ orchestration (source fetch, build control, artifact management, CI/GitHub Actions, packaging & release, environment diagnostics) plus the cmake/ superbuild engine and top-level config/docs.

nodes.summary: Parses a ninja build log into per-target statistics and timing events for later analysis.

nodes.name: compute_peak_concurrency_by_component
nodes.summary: Computes peak build concurrency per component from parsed timing events.

nodes.name: generate_summaries
nodes.summary: Generates the human-readable and machine-readable build resource summaries and report artifacts from collected logs.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs the instrumented build and produces resource-usage summaries.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Project configuration for the scan_tools helper package used within the build tooling.

nodes.name: setup_ccache.py
nodes.summary: Utility that generates a ccache configuration and emits environment variable exports to accelerate incremental ROCm builds.

nodes.name: gen_config
nodes.summary: Generates a ccache configuration file with the correct cache directory and compiler-check settings.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core routine that sets up the ccache directory and prints the environment exports for the shell to evaluate.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments and configures ccache for the current build environment.

nodes.name: setup_sccache_rocm.py
nodes.summary: Locates the sccache binary and emits shell environment settings (including HIP_CLANG_LAUNCHER) so that ROCm/HIP device compiles are routed through sccache for caching.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets HIP_CLANG_LAUNCHER because hipcc invokes clang by absolute path and bypasses the usual CMAKE compiler-launcher hooks.

nodes.name: find_sccache
nodes.summary: Resolves the path to an sccache executable, checking explicit overrides and PATH.

nodes.name: sccache_build_env
nodes.summary: Builds the dictionary of environment variables that configure sccache as the compiler and HIP clang launcher.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments, locates sccache, and prints shell-eval-able environment exports.

nodes.name: teatime.py
nodes.summary: A combination of 'tee' and 'time' for managing build logs: streams a subprocess's output to both the console and log files while tracking elapsed time.

nodes.name: OutputSink
nodes.summary: Fan-out sink that duplicates streamed process output to the terminal and to one or more log files.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Drives the tee/time behavior: reads from the input stream or child process and writes to all configured sinks.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments, sets up output sinks, and runs either a pipeline filter or a managed child command.

nodes.name: validate_shared_library.py
nodes.summary: Small utility that validates a shared library can be dynamically loaded via ctypes, used as a post-build smoke check.

nodes.name: custom_install_amdgpu_compiler.cmake
nodes.summary: Install-time CMake script that assembles and installs the AMDGPU compiler component files from the staging tree into the install prefix.

nodes.name: custom_install_amdgpu_runtime_dev.cmake
nodes.summary: Install-time CMake script that installs the AMDGPU runtime development files (headers and development artifacts) from the staging tree.

nodes.name: custom_install_amdgpu_runtime.cmake
nodes.summary: Install-time CMake script that selects and installs the dynamic runtime shared libraries for the amdgpu_hip_runtime component from the staging tree.

nodes.name: FindBLAS.cmake
nodes.summary: Custom CMake finder that resolves the virtual BLAS package for sub-projects, preferring the super-project's bundled host-blas (OpenBLAS) build.

nodes.name: FindLAPACK.cmake
nodes.summary: Custom CMake finder that resolves the virtual LAPACK package for sub-projects, deferring to the super-project's bundled host library.

nodes.name: find_config_version.tmpl.cmake
nodes.summary: configure_file template for a package version file that trampolines find_package version checks to a fixed super-project location.

nodes.name: find_config.tmpl.cmake
nodes.summary: configure_file template for a package config file that trampolines find_package() to redirect to a fixed super-project component directory.

nodes.name: therock_amdgpu_targets.cmake
nodes.summary: Declares the registry of AMDGPU gfx targets and their family groupings, maintaining global properties that map targets to families, product names, and per-project excludes.
nodes.languageNotes: Target metadata is stored as CMake GLOBAL properties keyed by target and family name.

nodes.name: therock_artifacts.cmake
nodes.summary: Provides the artifact bundling subsystem that slices the install tree into named artifact directories under build/artifacts for bootstrapping and CI/CD, and materializes local distributions.

nodes.name: therock_compiler_config.cmake
nodes.summary: Project-wide compiler configuration, including MSVC embedded debug database settings for ccache/sccache compatibility and bootstrap compiler validation on Windows.

nodes.name: therock_default_targets.cmake
nodes.summary: Defines convenience helper functions that create top-level 'therock-{name}' aggregate targets and their bare-name aliases used across the build system.

nodes.name: therock_detect_python_versions.cmake
nodes.summary: Detects available Python interpreters and whether they support linking against a shared libpython, driving Python-version-dependent build configuration.

nodes.name: therock_explicit_finders.cmake
nodes.summary: Overrides find_library/find_path within sub-projects to resolve only known super-project libraries, providing an opt-in safety net for legacy projects using naked find calls.

nodes.name: therock_external_source.cmake
nodes.summary: Provides therock_enable_external_source to let a subcomponent build from a user-provided external source directory instead of the vendored submodule.

nodes.name: therock_features.cmake
nodes.summary: Registry of build features (components and optional configurations) that can be enabled/disabled, tracking inter-feature dependencies and platform-based disables.

nodes.name: therock_flag_utils.cmake
nodes.summary: Centralized flag system controlling how variables and compiler defines propagate into included subprojects, distinct from the feature-inclusion system.

nodes.name: therock_global_post_subproject.cmake
nodes.summary: Hook run at the end of every sub-project's configure that applies super-project-level fixups across all defined targets and invokes any user post hook.

nodes.name: therock_globals.cmake
nodes.summary: Defines project-wide global condition variables (e.g. platform checks like THEROCK_CONDITION_IS_WINDOWS) reusable in sub-project cache variable expressions.

nodes.name: therock_install_linux_build_id_files.cmake
nodes.summary: Install-time script that splits debug info out of Linux binaries into a .build-id directory tree using their ELF Build ID, via objcopy and readelf.

nodes.name: therock_job_pools.cmake
nodes.summary: Configures Ninja job pools, sizing a background build job pool relative to the host CPU core count to throttle low-priority concurrent work.

nodes.name: therock_meson_env.cmake
nodes.summary: Assembles space-separated CC/CXX environment strings for Meson sub-projects from CMake's compiler and launcher variables (handling chained launchers).

nodes.name: therock_primlibs_benchmark_deps.cmake
nodes.summary: Workaround that injects find_package(amd_smi) so rocPRIM benchmarks get the amd_smi imported target and include directories they link against but never request.

nodes.name: therock_sanitizers.cmake
nodes.summary: Configures compiler/linker sanitizer stanzas (e.g. ASan) for subprojects, honoring a global THEROCK_SANITIZER setting with per-subproject overrides.
nodes.languageNotes: Single CMake function returning a sanitizer stanza and selection via out-parameters.

nodes.name: therock_subproject_dep_provider.cmake
nodes.summary: Injected find_package dependency provider that redirects subproject package resolution to super-project-provided directories, enforcing declared dependencies and pkg-config prefixes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CMake dependency provider hook (cmake_language SET_DEPENDENCY_PROVIDER) with cmake_policy scoping.

nodes.name: therock_subproject_utils.cmake
nodes.summary: Utility helpers for subproject builds: recursively collecting targets, setting install RPATHs, and patching linked library references.

nodes.name: therock_subproject.cmake
nodes.summary: Core superbuild engine defining and activating CMake subprojects with performant source tracking, dependency wiring, toolchain setup, and staging/dist orchestration. Analogous to ExternalProject but tailored for TheRock's large, editable super-project.
nodes.languageNotes: Large CMake module (~1900 lines) using GLOBAL properties, out-parameter conventions, and fingerprinting for incremental builds.

nodes.name: therock_testing.cmake
nodes.summary: Registers CTest tests that validate shared libraries under a common path can be dlopen-loaded, skipping validation for sanitizer builds.

nodes.name: FLAGS.cmake
nodes.summary: Central registry of TheRock build feature flags, declaring THEROCK_FLAG_* cache variables with defaults, subproject propagation, and branch-specific overrides.
nodes.languageNotes: Declarative flag registry invoking therock_declare_flag/therock_finalize_flags from therock_flag_utils.

nodes.name: therock_sanitizer_configure
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective sanitizer for a subproject and emits the corresponding compiler/linker flag stanza and selection into out-parameters.

nodes.name: therock_dependency_provider
nodes.summary: Dependency provider macro that intercepts find_package for super-project-provided packages, resolving them from injected package directories instead of the system.

nodes.name: therock_reparse_super_project_find_package
nodes.summary: Re-parses a super-project-provided package's find_package config from a given path so the subproject can consume its targets and variables.

nodes.name: therock_get_all_targets
nodes.summary: Recursively collects all build targets under a directory into an output variable.

nodes.name: therock_set_install_rpath
nodes.summary: Sets the install RPATH on targets so runtime dependencies resolve within the ROCm distribution layout.

nodes.name: therock_patch_linked_lib
nodes.summary: Patches linked library references on a target to point at the correct staged/dist library paths.

nodes.name: therock_subproject_log_command
nodes.summary: Builds a logged command wrapper for subproject build/configure steps so output is captured to log files.

nodes.name: therock_subproject_fetch
nodes.summary: Fetches subproject sources (git or archive) into the expected source tree, mirroring FetchContent semantics for the superbuild.

nodes.name: therock_cmake_subproject_declare
nodes.summary: Declares a CMake subproject, recording its sources, dependencies, cmake vars, and configuration as target properties for later activation.

nodes.name: therock_cmake_subproject_provide_package
nodes.summary: Registers a package that a subproject provides to downstream subprojects, mapping the package name to a relative install path.

nodes.name: therock_cmake_subproject_activate
nodes.summary: Activates a declared subproject by materializing its ExternalProject build/stage/dist steps, dependency stamps, toolchain, and staging rules. The central orchestration routine of the superbuild.

nodes.name: therock_cmake_subproject_build_test
nodes.summary: Sets up build-and-test steps for a subproject, wiring CTest integration into the superbuild's staged distribution.

nodes.name: therock_cmake_subproject_glob_c_sources
nodes.summary: Globs C/C++ source files for a subproject to establish configure-time dependencies that trigger rebuilds on source changes.

nodes.name: therock_subproject_merge_compile_commands
nodes.summary: Merges per-subproject compile_commands.json files into a combined database for IDE and tooling support.

nodes.name: _therock_cmake_subproject_build_env_pairs
nodes.summary: Assembles environment variable name/value pairs to inject into a subproject's build commands.

nodes.name: _therock_cmake_subproject_setup_deps
nodes.summary: Assembles the provided-package variables and dependency directories consumed by the injected dependency provider.

nodes.name: _therock_cmake_subproject_get_stage_dirs
nodes.summary: Resolves the stage directories for a subproject's declared dependencies.

nodes.name: _therock_cmake_subproject_deps_to_stamp
nodes.summary: Maps a subproject's dependencies to their build stamp files for incremental build ordering.

nodes.name: _therock_cmake_subproject_collect_build_deps
nodes.summary: Recursively collects the transitive build-time dependencies of a subproject.

nodes.name: _therock_cmake_subproject_collect_runtime_deps
nodes.summary: Recursively collects the transitive runtime dependencies of a subproject for dist merging.

nodes.name: _therock_filter_project_gpu_targets
nodes.summary: Filters the configured AMDGPU target list down to those applicable for a given subproject.

nodes.name: _therock_cmake_subproject_setup_toolchain
nodes.summary: Generates the toolchain and project_init configuration injected into a subproject, wiring compilers, sanitizers, GPU targets, and default cmake vars.

nodes.name: _therock_subproject_fprint_source_dir
nodes.summary: Computes a fingerprint of a subproject's source directory to detect changes and drive incremental rebuilds.

nodes.name: therock_test_validate_shared_lib
nodes.summary: Adds a CTest test validating that named shared libraries under a common path can be dlopen-loaded, skipping sanitizer builds.

nodes.name: open_archive_for_read
nodes.summary: Opens a tar archive for reading, auto-detecting .tar.zst or .tar.xz compression from the file extension.

nodes.name: gha_warn_if_not_running_on_ci
nodes.summary: Logs a warning if the CI environment variable is not set, indicating the code may not be running under GitHub Actions.

nodes.name: gha_add_to_path
nodes.summary: Adds an entry to the system PATH for subsequent GitHub Actions workflow steps by appending to the $GITHUB_PATH file.

nodes.name: gha_set_env
nodes.summary: Sets environment variables for subsequent GitHub Actions workflow steps by appending to the $GITHUB_ENV file.

layers.name: Superbuild CMake Engine
layers.description: CMake modules and feature-flag registries that drive the ROCm super-project configure/build/stage/dist phases, including the root CMakeLists.txt, FLAGS.cmake, and the cmake/therock_*.cmake infrastructure.

layers.name: Source Fetch & Topology
layers.description: Scripts and manifests that fetch git submodules and external sources, apply patches, and translate BUILD_TOPOLOGY.toml into CMake targets and version manifests.

layers.name: Build Orchestration
layers.description: Top-level build drivers and instrumentation: buildctl, portable Linux container builds, ccache/sccache setup, venv provisioning, compile-commands merging, and build-time/resource monitoring.

layers.name: Artifact Management
layers.description: The _therock_utils shared package (artifact catalog, S3/local storage backends, pattern-matching, archiving) plus CLIs that fetch, index, and install ROCm build artifacts.

layers.name: CI / GitHub Actions Orchestration
layers.description: GitHub Actions matrix generation, workflow API clients, upload/publish helpers, per-library CI test-executable runners, secret scanning, and one-off CI hack scripts.

layers.name: Packaging & Release
layers.description: Debian/RPM/wheel packaging templates and generators under build_tools/packaging plus the wheel-build entry point and release promotion/upload automation.

layers.name: Environment Diagnostics
layers.description: Host diagnostics toolkit: the env_check framework (tool/device/GPU checks), the diagnose entry point, health-status reporting, and shared-library/Windows install validators.

layers.name: Project Config & Docs
layers.description: Top-level project documentation (README, CONTRIBUTING, ROADMAP, SUPPORTED_GPUS), Python/tooling config (requirements, pre-commit, gitleaks, pyproject, CMakePresets, version.json), and editor/agent rules.

tour.title: Project Overview & Purpose
tour.description: Start here to understand what TheRock is: a lightweight CMake super-project that builds HIP and ROCm from source across Linux and native Windows, producing nightly ROCm and PyTorch releases. The README lays out the quick-start flow (fetch submodules, configure with CMake, build with Ninja) and the feature flags that gate which components compile, while the roadmap frames where the project is heading. Keep this build flow in mind: every later step maps onto one stage of it.

tour.title: The Superbuild Entry Point
tour.description: The root CMakeLists.txt is where a configured build actually begins: it includes TheRock's CMake modules, detects the target AMDGPU families and Python versions, and hands off to the build orchestration. CMakePresets.json gives developers reusable configure/build recipes so everyone shares consistent setups, and version.json is the single source of truth pinning the ROCm version the build produces. Together these three files answer 'how does the build start and what version am I building?'

tour.title: Fetching the Source Tree
tour.description: Before anything compiles, the ROCm component repositories must be materialized. fetch_sources.py is the primary entry point that initializes git submodules, resolves external git sources according to the build topology, applies patches, and pulls large files for the selected source sets. It leans on fetch_repo.py to check out each repo at a precise branch, commit, or PR, and reads .gitmodules, the manifest declaring every ROCm component repository (rocm-systems, rocm-libraries, and friends).

tour.title: Build Topology to CMake
tour.description: TheRock's component dependency graph lives in one declarative file, BUILD_TOPOLOGY.toml, which describes every component, its build metadata, and how they depend on each other. build_topology.py parses that TOML into a rich model of submodules, source sets, build stages, artifact groups, and artifacts, exposing dependency ordering used across the tooling. topology_to_cmake.py then code-generates the actual CMake targets, dependency variables, and build order from that model — this is the bridge from declarative config to the imperative build.
tour.languageLesson: TOML is used here as a declarative dependency manifest: a single, human-editable file becomes the source of truth for a generated build graph. This 'config-as-data, code-generate-the-rest' pattern keeps the component topology reviewable in one place instead of scattered across CMake.

tour.title: The Superbuild CMake Engine
tour.description: therock_subproject.cmake is the heart of the build system — it defines and activates each ROCm component as a CMake subproject with source tracking, dependency wiring, toolchain setup, and staging/dist orchestration, acting like a purpose-built ExternalProject for a large, editable super-project. FLAGS.cmake and therock_features.cmake form the feature-flag registry that decides which components and options are enabled and how those choices propagate to subprojects. therock_amdgpu_targets.cmake maps gfx targets to GPU families, connecting the -DTHEROCK_AMDGPU_FAMILIES flag from Step 2 to real build configuration.
tour.languageLesson: TheRock's subproject engine is analogous to CMake's ExternalProject but tuned for a super-project where subcomponents remain editable in place. Each component still flows through the standard build phases — configure, build, stage, dist — while the engine tracks sources and wires cross-component dependencies.

tour.title: Driving & Accelerating the Build
tour.description: With sources fetched (Step 3) and CMake configured (Steps 4-5), buildctl.py is the CLI that controls the super-project build: enabling or disabling components, reconfiguring CMake, and bootstrapping from prebuilt artifacts. setup_venv.py provisions the Python environment the tooling runs in, while setup_ccache.py generates a ccache configuration to make incremental compiles dramatically faster. linux_portable_build.py wraps the whole build in a manylinux-style container to produce broadly compatible Linux artifacts.
tour.languageLesson: ccache accelerates rebuilds by caching compiler outputs keyed on preprocessed input; for a project this large it turns hour-long recompiles into minutes. It is wired in via CMAKE_C/CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER, so the compiler invocation is transparently wrapped without changing build rules.

tour.title: Artifact Management: _therock_utils
tour.description: A build this large produces its outputs as versioned artifacts, and the shared _therock_utils package is the reusable core behind that. artifacts.py catalogs available artifacts and materializes them into a target tree, while pattern_match.py is the glob engine that selects files by include/exclude rules and copies them while preserving hardlinks and symlinks. fileset_tool.py exposes these as a low-level CLI, and artifact_manager.py is the high-level, high-fan-out driver that fetches, pushes, copies, and inspects artifacts across local and S3 backends, driven by the same build topology from Step 4.

tour.title: CI & GitHub Actions Orchestration
tour.description: TheRock's CI has to fan a build out across many GPU families and platforms, and github_actions_api.py — the single most depended-upon module in this graph — is the shared client for authenticated GitHub REST/gh-cli calls, workflow queries, and CI environment I/O. amdgpu_family_matrix.py is the central registry mapping GPU families to runner labels and variants, and configure_multi_arch_ci.py is the decision engine that reads workflow inputs and git context to expand jobs into per-platform, per-family build configurations. workflow_outputs.py defines the canonical storage layout for everything a workflow run produces, tying CI back to the artifact backends from Step 7.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions matrix builds expand a compact set of dimensions (here: GPU family x platform x variant) into many parallel jobs. TheRock generates that matrix dynamically in Python rather than hardcoding it in YAML, so the family registry stays the single place new hardware is added.

tour.title: Packaging & Release
tour.description: Once artifacts exist, they get repackaged for distribution. build_python_packages.py orchestrates building ROCm Python wheels from artifacts (supporting both the kpack-split and legacy flows), while build_package.py drives native Linux .deb/.rpm packaging of host, device, meta, and per-gfx-arch variants from build manifests. The how_to_do_release runbook ties it all together as the human-facing, step-by-step procedure for actually cutting and publishing a ROCm/TheRock release.
tour.languageLesson: Python wheels bundle prebuilt binaries so users install ROCm with pip instead of compiling. Splitting a huge SDK across multiple wheels (core, devel, libraries, per-arch device packages) keeps individual downloads manageable while a thin meta-package pulls in the right combination.

tour.title: Environment Diagnostics
tour.description: The final stop is the toolkit that tells a developer why a build or install went wrong. diagnose.py is a lightweight CLI that renders the banner and runs the environment/tool checklist, and check_tools.py is the framework behind it — a family of Check* classes that validate the presence and versions of Python, CMake, compilers, OS, CPU, and disk, rendering pass/warn/fail lines. health_status.py is the top-level entry point that dumps full system info and runs an OS-aware ordered set of toolchain checks, closing the loop from 'what do I need?' (Step 1) to 'is my machine actually ready?'
